entry.title: Re: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03
entry.content: I got the idea from the code, but in which method we need to call is code is not clear, if you can share the github repo containing all artifacts for this would be much helpfull

entry.title: Wildcard incorrectly restricting data on a webi report
entry.content: I'm not sure if this is the right spot to post this but I am need of help on a webi report I've created. I have a universe that has a single table with 20 columns containing data on projects such as names, project codes, locations, etc.. On my webi report, I'm using 5 dimensions setup as "Matches Pattern" with the default value set as a wildcard. So for example one of the filters is on the mgr. Default value is set as the wildcard and regardless if there is a set manager or not within the data...it returns all data for the specific project. However, I have 1 filter for a project code that when the default value is left as the wildcard - NO data is returned for a project that has no value in the project code column. It ONLY returns data if a WBS Code is selected. Shouldn't the wildcard not restrict data when used? Otherwise, I am lost on why its restricting data.

entry.title: Re: F3445 Maintain PIRs
entry.content:
Check for Notes on App "F3445" first ...

Perhaps Notes like 3482218 - F3445: Wrong handling of version indicator in excel upload with special characters - SAP for Me can solve the issue ...? Do you use the App in a S/4 System with an Older Fiori UI-version / specific Language / use of Special Chars?

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Comments for Multiple Candidates
entry.content:
Our recruiters are wondering if it's possible to add the same comment to multiple candidates at the same time. For example, there are times when they want to add a comment like "interviewed at job fair on Feb 24, 2026" to 10+ candidates. It would be a nice-to-have feature to allow them to put a checkmark next to those employees in the list and then add that same comment to all of them at the same time to save some time. They are beginning to use the new Reimagined Applicant view more often. 

Any ideas if something like this is possible? Thanks!

entry.title: Learning and Development Reporting
entry.content:
Is there reporting available for Learning and Development Module. 

We are trying to create dashboards to provide to employees to provide metrics on training completion and participation.  

Is there anything available? 

Our current thought is to back feed the data into a power bi report, but I would imagine there has to be something available in Success Factors natively.

entry.title: Modify the status of courses in bulk in LMS
entry.content:
Good afternoon, team,

I am writing to share the following issue with the LMS tool.

We are currently undergoing a company restoration process, therefore, it is important to bulk update the status of the courses or learning activities created in the learning tool.

Reference image attached.

 

I look forward to your response and thank you in advance.

Sincerely,

entry.title: Re: What is best way to integrate VS2022 with existing CR...
entry.content:
Don is right, SAP does not support NuGet for Crystal. Install the latest Service Pack of SAP Crystal Reports from SAP and reference the assemblies from that install. Mixing CR 11 binaries with newer runtime libraries is what’s causing the dependency conflicts.

The SDK will load CR 11 reports just fine.

Given your scenario (same schema across databases), you have two solid options:

  • Dynamically set the DB connection at runtime via ReportDocument, or

  • Fetch the data yourself and push a DataTable into the report using SetDataSource().

If it is a single-table report, pushing the dataset is usually the cleaner and more stable approach.

entry.title: Re: Time Off validation on start date
entry.content: Thanks VR - that is an interesting function. For example I start the leave on a Saturday and end on Tuesday where Sat is a non working day, what will this return?

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Media Product Hierarchy with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content: How do you do for box set? Also what is method to allocate revenue and cost of boxset to individual title level ?

entry.title: Careers Tab Permissions and External/Internal Candidate Conversion
entry.content: Hi, we have a legal business requirement to restrict access to our Internal Careers Tab for some of our employees. We updated the permissions accordingly, but didn't realize that this permission drives the proper conversion of External to Internal candidates. What we are seeing now is that when our external candidates are hired, a second internal candidate profile is being created instead of converting the external to internal. Now we have a significant number of employees who have both an internal and an external candidate profile. Has anyone had the same requirement and found a way to both restrict permissions to the Careers Tab and convert the external profile to an internal profile?

entry.title: Re: How to set default values in Input elements on the Cr...
entry.content: @MioYasukate - That is to auto populate for an action . But how to default values in an object page for normal input fields. Do you have any example?

entry.title: Re: Where did the BTP ABAP certification (C_ABAPD) go?
entry.content: este examen ya aparece disponible, saber como va hacer el formato nuevo , por sistema o por IA ?

entry.title: Re: IDOCs are Still Safe for SAP S/4HANA - SAP Clean Core Extensibility Level B
entry.content: @rajeshps AFAIK in public cloud you have no IDOC so this stands validated for Private and On premise installations only as Type B.

entry.title: How to change the extractor collection frequency to hourly in CSA for certificate stores
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We would like to know, how to change the extractor frequency in FRUN CSA for certain config stores like PSE certificate.
Currently our FRUN is at 5.0 version and we see the collection frequency defined as daily.
We want it to change to hourly across the FRUN system (only for certificate related stores) for the alerts to auto close.


Thanks and regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change the extractor collection frequency to h...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To change the data collection frequency of a specific extractor in CSA (Configuration & Security Analysis), follow the steps outlined in the KBA below:

  • 3721048 - How to Change Extractor Collection Frequency in CSA - SAP Focused Run - SAP for Me


P.S: Please note that after applying the changes, it may take up to one hour for them to be used by the system.


Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Fixed asset: Asset Class without Useful Life
entry.content: Hello folks, Thank you for your advices. I couldn't take much time on this afterward. I decided to put just 1 in the useful life. It might make some invevience in reporting in the future but I don't know yet.

entry.title: Re: Mismatch between ML Control Rec. and ML Period Totals...
entry.content: You may  have interrupted CKMLCP during processing,which caused an in consistency between the Material Ledger control record and the period totals data.You can try running CKMDUVMAT to repair the data and then attempt the posting again.

entry.title: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content:
The Problem

AI coding assistants have transformed development, but ABAP developers are stuck manually copying code, activating objects, and feeding errors back to the AI.

The Solution

abapgit-agent bridges AI tools (Claude Code, Copilot, etc.) with your ABAP system:

AI generates code → Push to git → Agent pulls & activates → Returns errors → AI fixes
 

Features

  • pull - Pull from git and activate objects
  • inspect - Syntax check via Code Inspector
  • unit - Run AUnit tests
  • view / preview - Explore tables, classes, CDS views

Quick Start

npm install -g abapgit-agent
cd your-abap-repo
abapgit-agent init --folder /src/ --package ZMY_PACKAGE
# Edit .abapGitAgent with credentials
abapgit-agent pull

Prerequisites: abapGit (Developer version), Node.js 16+

Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/SylvosCai/abapgit-agent
- Docs: https://github.com/SylvosCai/abapgit-agent/blob/master/INSTALL.md

Feedback welcome!

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content: Full documentation is available at: https://sylvoscai.github.io/abapgit-agent/

entry.title: SAP Support Potalのセキュリティパッチデーが更新されていない
entry.content:
ご担当各位
お世話になっております。


SAPサポートポータルのナレッジベースを見たところ、セキュリティパッチデーの欄において、パッチ発行予定日および発行実績がDIGITS年11月から更新されていないことに気づきました。

URL:
セキュリティパッチ発行予定は、更新されないのが正しい表記となるでしょうか？
上記の他に更新されているページ、或いは英語版で更新されたページがあれば、ご教授ください。

なお、本件について CIC に問い合わせたところ、SAP for Meにて問い合わせるよう回答をいただいておりました。

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Support Potalのセキュリティパッチデーが更新されていない
entry.content:
担当部署に確認したところ、現時点の方針では、日本語に翻訳されたHotNewsの情報は今後提供されず、

したがいまして、日本語翻訳された重要ノート (HotNews) 情報のページは更新されないとのことです。その旨ご了承ください。

新規・更新されたHotNewsノートにつきましては、SAP for Meのアプリケーション  https://me.sap.com/app/hotnews の利用をご検討ください。

(KBA 2342391 - SAP HotNews アプリケーションへのアクセスおよび使用方法 - SAP for Me - SAP for Me)

さらに、SAP for Meの通知機能をご利用いただくことも可能です。

通知設定については、2530034 - 電子メール、SMS、および/または SAP for Me 通知の管理方法 - SAP for Me - SAP for Me をご確認ください。

特定の月にリリースされたHotNewsをSAP HotNewsアプリケーションにて確認していただき、日本語に翻訳されていないものがありましたら、日本語翻訳をご依頼ください。

翻訳依頼方法の詳細につきましては、2595923 - SAP Notes および SAP KBA サービス情報: 日本語 - SAP for Meをご参照いただければと存じます。

entry.title: Missing Communications and notifications
entry.content:
I currently have subscriptions to get notifications for SAP Ariba Procurement, SAP Business Network, SAP Ariba Sourcing, SAP API Management, SAP Fieldglass, SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime, and SAP - SAP Cloud Connector.

I would have expected that any degradation with relation to SAP Ariba Procurement but when * occurred I was not notified.  After consulting with other team members they did not receive emails as well.

Charter team needs to know how we can automatically be notified when items such as * occur.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Missing Emails

Unknown steps to reproduce.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing Communications and notifications
entry.content:
Because notifications like * are very special, it is documented in the KBA 3624413 - How to subscribe to Functional Degradation notifications for Ariba products - SAP for Me as following:

Email notifications for Functional Degradations are only sent to users who subscribe to the ongoing event via Get Support application. 

That is to say this type of notification can only be obtained through manual customer subscription. This is a standard design feature of the Ariba product, and its triggering logic is different from other events.

entry.title: Re: Technical AS-IS EA Canvas: Accelerating Assessments in Undocumented Environments
entry.content: Nice way to make the discovery more engaging and meaning full, helps even more in the scenario where there is no As-Is documentation in place. Bringing in right stakeholder is a critical success factor through. Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Re: Bienvenidos al grupo de SAP Community Mexico
entry.content:
Hola, soy Abril 👋 👸

Llevo casi 20 años en el mundo del reclutamiento y prácticamente toda mi vida profesional he estado ligada a SAP. Me ha tocado ver consultores crecer desde perfiles junior hasta convertirse en arquitectos, líderes de proyecto o directores… y eso es algo que disfruto muchísimo.

Me apasiona conectar a personas con oportunidades que realmente hagan sentido para su momento de vida, no solo profesionalmente, sino también personal. Siempre he creído que un buen match no es solo técnico, también es humano y hasta energético ✨

¿En qué parte de México estás basada, o a dónde viajas?

Soy de Monterrey, NL, pero desde hace más de 10 años vivo en San Miguel de Allende, Gto. Eso me ha permitido equilibrar el ritmo corporativo con una vida más consciente.

Trabajo de manera remota 👩‍💻y viajo solo cuando algún proyecto lo requiere o por placer 🏖.

¿En qué área estás trabajando hoy?

Principalmente en reclutamiento especializado SAP (perfiles senior, arquitectos y posiciones estratégicas).

Aunque en los últimos años también he estado reclutando en otras industrias, y eso me ha hecho aprender muchísimo sobre distintos modelos de negocio, culturas organizacionales y estilos de liderazgo. Todos los días aprendo algo nuevo, y eso me encanta. 💫

¿Qué estás aprendiendo o qué te da curiosidad ahora?

Estoy aprendiendo a usar mejor SAP Learning Hub, explorando la herramienta para entender cómo puede ayudar a los consultores a certificarse, actualizarse y seguir creciendo en el ecosistema SAP. Me interesa mucho acompañarlos no solo a encontrar oportunidades, sino a prepararse estratégicamente para ellas.

¿Un interés fuera de SAP?

El yoga y la meditación son parte esencial de mi vida 🧘🏻‍♀️
Me ayudan a mantener claridad, equilibrio y enfoque en un entorno que suele ser muy acelerado.

También me apasiona todo lo relacionado con crecimiento personal y conciencia.

Estoy aquí para conectar, aprender, compartir y seguir construyendo relaciones reales, no solo contactos. 🤍

Gracias por este espacio @MariettaLopez , Edgar y Sandra! 👏

entry.title: RBP Troubleshooting Tool Issue
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm using the RBP Troubleshooting tool to compare permissions between 2 Users and it's showing them as matching. Upon 'Showing All' and clicking on one of the Roles it takes me to the Permission Role as expected. However the Permission Groups Granted and Target that it is showing in that Permission Role is wrong. Neither of the Users are in either the Granted nor Target Groups.
They are in a different Permission Group that is assigned to that same Permission Role, but not the one it was showing me. It seems like a fatal-flaw with the RBP Troubleshooting tool if it's meant to be narrowing down the RBP issues, but showing you the wrong Matched Assignment Groups.

Has anyone else come across this? 

Is this a SAP error that needs fixing?

Many thanks for your advice!

entry.title: Sorry, it is not possible to maintain payment information if there is no job information or .....
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Good day!

We are now migrating our ONB to 2.0 and find the below issue while also adding payment info as requirement.

May I kindly ask if there's already a concrete resolution for this issue?

I've noticed that there's a patch but why is the error still existent? 

I also checked this KBAs, however, no resolution indicated.

  • 3219782 - Payment Information validation error in MPH or NHDR - Onboarding
  • 3673875 - Error when changing payment information start date in add new employee - Employee Central
  • 3194252 - [Onboarding] Sorry, it is not possible to maintain payment information if there is no job information or no country/region in job information.

Thank you very much in advance for the help and inputs.

 

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Need to be able to download SAP Ariba Compliance Documents
entry.content:
We need to download SAP Ariba compliance documents such as SOC 1 from our SAP for Me account. Could you assist with this request?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to be able to download SAP Ariba Compliance Docu...
entry.content:
To access the 'Find Compliance Documents' card, the 'Access Compliance Documents authorization' must be assigned to your S-user ID. 

For security purposes, this authorization can only be granted by your company's Super Administrator(s). You can find their contact information on the  Important Contacts page.

Additionally, you may find the below KBA helpful, as it provides detailed information about the S-user ID authorizations required for the 'Find Compliance Documents' card. 

3629697 - S-user ID authorizations required for Compliance Documents Finder applications - SAP for Me

Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA Custom Measure
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

It is recommended to create a new custom "Days to Offer" measure instead of updating the calculation of the existing "Days to Fill" and "Days to Hire" measures as they might be used for deriving other Result Measure (eg. Time to Fill per Hire etc.) .

You can either engage your CSP/ CEE or a qualified partner to implement the new custom measure.

Thank you.

Kind Regards,

Wei Cher Chen

entry.title: Library Results Booster - Tiers & Weight
entry.content:
Hello

I'm keen to hear from those who have used the Learning Library's search results booster features: Search Tiers and/or Search Weight. 

What worked/didn't work for you? How did you set it up? Did you use something else and did that work better/worse? 

Any and all responses appreciated. 

Thank you in advance.

Sarah

entry.title: SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription
entry.content:
SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription

Also, can you share a documentation on how the SAP for Me roles work? What do they enable or control? Thank you.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Steps:
1. SAP for ME
2. Systems & Provisioning
3. Get Notified
4. Add New Subscription
5. Contact field is greyed out

Also, can you share a documentation on how the SAP for Me roles work? What do they enable or control? Thank you.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription
entry.content:
The contact field greyed out is currently unavailable because you are viewing the My Subscriptions section. This particular view is designed to allow you to manage or add new subscriptions exclusively for your own S-user. 
 
If you need to manage subscriptions for other S-users, please switch to the Manage Subscriptions view. For further guidance, you can refer to the KBA 2896942 - How Super and Cloud Administrators manage Cloud Notification subscriptions for other users - SAP for Me

For detailed steps on how individual S-users can manage their own Cloud Notification subscriptions, refer to KBA 2900069 - How S-users manage their own Cloud Notification subscriptions - SAP for Me
If you have further questions or need additional assistance, please don’t hesitate to reach out.  
Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support  

Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support

entry.title: Resolving Key Mapping Missing Issues while sending Freight Unit from Cloud ERP to On Premise system
entry.content:
Introduction

The process for Scope Item 6W1 for External Transportation Planning enables transportation requests created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP to be planned within an SAP On Premise system. In this case, Freight units generated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP can be transferred to the On Premise system (with SAP TM) for detailed transportation planning.

Once planning is completed, the results are sent back and integrated into SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP in the form of freight orders. Upon transfer, transportation charges for these freight orders are calculated automatically. Based on the finalized freight orders, outbound deliveries can then be initiated.

This post covers the scenario where an error is received and the possible workaround for the error.

Kindly note that below steps are a workaround and steps must be taken to resolve error by undertaking the master data replication as per the set up guide.

Process Details

Complete the set up for the scenario including the set up of communication scenario and Data replication. The process requires the Purchase order be created within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP. Ensure that the Purchase group is selected as "006" External TM Planning (refer below screenshot).

Based on settings a Transportation Management Freight unit is created. The same can be seen within the Purchase order app under the tab "TM Status"

As the Process needs the Freight Unit planning within the external TM system the scope items 6W1 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP should be activated. Once done the Freight Unit from Public Cloud system is replicated to the On Premise system.

This replication message should create a Freight Unit within the On Premise / Decentralized TM system.

Challenge

In case of Failure of the FU replication, check the message within the SAP Message Monitor to identify the type of error. One of the error that can occur may relate to Invalid data. As you can see in the below screenshot the error related to invalid data.

This blog covers on the process to be followed to resolve this issue.

To resolve the issue, as you can see the below warnings need to be removed to resolve the error. These warning relate to incorrect / missing key mapping for different Master Data Objects like Location / Business Partner / Product between the Public Cloud and the On Premise system. This can happen in case the replication was not performed correctly.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, key mapping needs to be maintained in both the Public Cloud and the On Premise system.

You can follow the following steps for the same.

  1. Use the Tcode MDG_KM_MAINTAIN within the On Premise SAP system. Manage Key Mapping UI opens.

  1. For each of the impacted Business Object Type (as per the error) select the Object Type (example Business Partner in above screenshot) and Object Type ID, Business System and the Master Data ID (example Business partner ID).
  2. Click on Add Row to add the entries as below. Fill in the details such that there are 2 rows each of Public cloud and On Premise system
    1. Row 1 with Public Cloud System ID and BP number and Row 2 for BP UUID in the standard format: 8-4-4-4-12 (hexadecimal groupings). Example – If location UUID is 355F4B6A67ED1EEE9297A3E7521BF064 it should be mentioned as 355F4B6A-67ED-1EEE-9297-A3E7521BF064
    2. Repeat the same for the On Premise system with corresponding details added in row 3 & 4.

 

  1. Save
  2. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 4 for all other Master data objects as per the error.
  3. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 5 within the Public Cloud system using the app „Manage Key Mapping“.

Rerun the process of FU replication and mapping error should be resolved now.

entry.title: Re: The Joule Studio Learning Series: Lead Agentic Development with Confidence
entry.content: highly informative and delivered with clarity, thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration (CPI/HCI) || JDBC Adapter (Part 1) || Everything you need to know, A step-
entry.content:
Hi all,

Wherever the JDBC Driver upload is failing even when you’re downloading the jar files from their official sites, it is because of incompatibility of the version with SAP CPI.

To make it easy you can refer to this SAP Help document where you can find and download the compatible version:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configure-jdbc-drivers?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 

Regards

Ravi

entry.title: Re: Navigating the Future: Understanding Upcoming Changes to SAP Universal ID
entry.content: Por favor me pueden ayudar ¿Porque en el apartado de "Actividad" solamente aparece "completado" pero no aparece el apartado de "En Progreso"? estoy tomando unos cursos pero no se listan, gracias

entry.title: Re: What contains under unused_data of disk_overview query
entry.content:
This is allocated disk space inside data volumes but not actively used by tables.
Common reasons:
Deleted records (space not yet reclaimed)
Table reorganizations
Archiving activities
Delta merge leftovers
Fragmentation in persistence layer

Unused ≠ Free OS disk space.
It’s still reserved by HANA internally until defragmentation/reclaim happens.

entry.title: For one employee:/3F1 (EE PF) = 249/3F5 = 249/552 (Total Deduction) = 498/3F5 not to be add
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working in India Payroll (Country Grouping 40) in SAP HCM.
Schema: LN00 (custom)
PCR: ZNK3 (copied from standard INK3)
Issue
For one employee:

/3F1 (EE PF) = 249

/3F5 = 249

/552 (Total Deduction) = 498

But:

Net Pay is correct

Only Total Deduction is doubled

entry.title: Multiple Address Handling
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are using SAP Cloud ERP and have business partners that operate multiple factories and plants. We are currently debating whether to register them as a single BP using the "Multiple Address Handling" feature or to create a separate BP for each plant.
Since these partners act as both customers and suppliers, I would like to know if Multiple Address Handling is still unavailable for the supplier (vendor) function?

Best Regards,
Saki

entry.title: Re: to stop deducting pf of employees
entry.content: Hi to all Hello Experts, I am working in India Payroll (Country Grouping 40) in SAP HCM. Schema: LN00 (custom) PCR: ZNK3 (copied from standard INK3) Issue For one employee: /3F1 (EE PF) = 249 /3F5 = 249 /552 (Total Deduction) = 498 But: Net Pay is correct Only Total Deduction is doubled

entry.title: Joule Studio Enablement Guide
entry.content:
📑 Table of Contents

  1. Overview
  2. Use Case & Mission Description
  3. Prerequisites
  4. Setting Up SAP Build for Joule Studio
  5. Required Subscriptions & Instances in BTP
  6. Mandatory Roles & Role Collections
  7. Ensuring Required Subscriptions Are Available
  8. Adding Missing Cloud Identity Service (Additional Tenant)
  9. Formation Creation (Mandatory for Joule Integration)
  10. Enablement Complete

 

1. Overview

Joule Studio is SAP’s platform to build Joule Agents, Joule Skills, and integrate enterprise AI capabilities directly into SAP applications. It is part of SAP Build, enabling teams to create and extend business applications and AI solutions with built-in governance and clean core alignment.

 

2. Use Case & Mission Description

  • Joule Studio is activated
  • Required BTP services are set up
  • Identity and trust between Joule and IAS are configured
  • Administrators can deploy Joule agents
  • Developers can create Joule Skills, Document Grounding, and integrate custom APIs

3. Prerequisites

To activate Joule Studio, the following must be fulfilled:

Required Entitlements

  • SAP Build Process Automation — Subscriptions(build_default)
  • Joule — Subscriptions(standard)
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services — Subscriptions(additional-tenant)

Cloud Identity Services (Mandatory)

IAS is required for:

  • Authentication
  • SSO
  • Authorization management
  • Trust configuration for Joule

If you do not have IAS, you must create a new tenant.

 

4. Setting Up SAP Build for Joule Studio

Standalone activation of:

  • Joule
  • SAP Build Process Automation

 

SAP BTP Boosters/Subscriptions

Boosters automate service activation.
Navigate:

Global Account → Boosters or Create Subscriptions of each of the plans given.

Use the following:

  • “Set Up SAP Build Process Automation”
  • “Set Up Joule”

Each booster/Subscriptions:

  • Assigns roles
  • Creates role collections
  • Subscribes necessary services

 

5. Required Subscriptions & Instances in BTP

Mandatory

Service

Plan

Required For

SAP Build Process Automation

build_default

Joule Studio backend

Joule

standard

Joule Studio AI

SAP Cloud Identity Services

Additional-Tenant

Authentication, SSO

 

6. Mandatory Roles & Role Collections

The Joule Booster does NOT create a role collection.

You MUST manually create one:

Create Role Collection

  1. Subaccount → Security → Role Collections
  2. Click Create
  3. Name it: Joule_Role_Collection

Add Roles

Navigate to:
Security → Role Collections → Joule_Role_Collection → Edit

Add:

  • extensibility_developer
  • end_user

Save.

Assign to Your User

Security → Users
Assign: Joule_Role_Collection

 

7. Ensure All Required Subscriptions Are Visible in the Systems

Under:
Global Account → System Landscape → Systems

Confirm:

  • Joule (standard)
  • SAP Build Process Automation (build_default)
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services (Additional Tenant)*

(*) If missing, add manually (next section).

 

8. Adding Cloud Identity Service (Additional Tenant)

If IAS additional tenant is not visible:

Navigate:

Global Account → System Landscape → Systems → Service Owner View

Click Add System

Enter:

  • System Type: SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • System Name: any name
  • CLD Tenant ID: IAS tenant ID

Finding CLD Tenant ID

Use Cloud Reporting:

🔹 Live Landscape
https://reporting.ondemand.com/sap/crp/cdo?query=xxx&type=crp_search

🔹 Canary Landscape
https://spc-vlab.ondemand.com/sap/crp/cdo?query=xxx&type=crp_search

Replace xxx with your IAS tenant ID.

⏳Note: It may take up to 24 hours for reporting to sync newly created IAS tenants.

 

9. Formation Creation (Mandatory for Joule Integration)

Navigate:
Subaccount → System Landscape → Formations → Create Formation

1. General Information

  • Formation Name: Your Choice
  • Formation Type: Integration with Joule

2. Include Systems

Add:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • Joule
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services

3. Integration Details

Check the box:
Enable Capability Deployment

4. Create

System may take time (~10–30 minutes) to reach Ready status.

This deployment links Joule, BPA, and IAS together.

 

 

10. Enablement Complete — You Can Build Joule Agents

Once the Formation is Ready:

You can now:

  • Build Joule Agents
  • Build Joule Skills
  • Use Document Grounding
  • Call Custom APIs

Thank you for your interest, understanding and patience.

entry.title: Entitlements
entry.content:
Introduction : 

In the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), Entitlements play a crucial role in defining how services and resources are allocated across different environments. They act as the foundation for enabling platform capabilities, ensuring that organizations can control usage based on purchased or subscribed service plans.

Understanding Entitlements becomes even more important when managing large landscapes that include multiple Global Accounts, Directories, and Subaccounts. This is where Entity Assignments and Service Assignments come into the picture. Entity Assignments allow administrators to distribute entitled service plans to specific subaccounts or directories, while Service Assignments focus on enabling and configuring those services within a given subaccount.

In this blog, we will explore the concept of Entitlements in SAP BTP and take a closer look at how Entity Assignments and Service Assignments work together to ensure proper service availability, governance, and resource management across the platform.




1 What is  Entitlements ?          

Entitlements in SAP BTP are the authorized usage of specific SAP BTP services and service plans within a global account

2 Types of Entitlements in SAP BTP ?

1 Entity Assignment

2 Service Assignment

 1 Entity Assignment ?

Entity Assignment is the process of assigning entitlements from the Global Account to a Subaccount.

Why Entity Assignment is Needed?

Because:

  • Entitlements are owned by Global Account
  • Applications are run inside Subaccounts
  • So you must assign first, then use.

 2 Service Assignment?

Service Assignment is assigning a specific service plan to a Subaccount or Space so developers can create instances.

How to Navigate to Entity Assignment

  • First we need to login BTP Cockpit
  • After click on Go To Your Trial Account shown in below.
    • After we can see Entitlements here

      => After we need to click on Entity Assignments

           Here we need to mention our Subaccounts/Directories:

      And here we can see services

      => After we will click on Edit Button
      we can see Add Service Plans Button here

      • Here Click on Add Service Plans Button

      After we can see Add service Plans  here select one service and click on Add service Plans Button

      • After in search bar search which service you need to add

                 This is all about Entity Assignment

        Service Assignment:

        When you click on Service Assignment we can see all available service here


        Conclusion : 

        Entitlements form the backbone of service management in SAP BTP by controlling which services and plans are available within an organization’s landscape. Through Entity Assignments, administrators can strategically distribute these entitlements to directories and subaccounts based on project, team, or business requirements. Service Assignments then take this a step further by enabling the actual consumption and configuration of services within specific subaccounts.

        By clearly understanding the relationship between Entitlements, Entity Assignments, and Service Assignments, organizations can ensure optimal resource utilization, maintain governance, and avoid unnecessary service limitations. Proper entitlement management not only improves operational efficiency but also supports scalable and secure cloud adoption within SAP BTP environments.

entry.title: Principal propagation setup as SAP Java backend and SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Principal Propagation Setup as Java Backend

SAP BTP =>Cloud Connector =>SAP NetWeaver AS Java

  1. Introduction

This blog explains how to configure principal propagation using X.509 certificate for an SAP NetWeaver AS Java backend (HTTPS). SAP Cloud Connector generates a client certificate for the user and forward it to the backend system.

  1. Architecture Overview

User =>BTP App/Work Zone =>BTP Destination (PrincipalPropagation) => SCC => AS Java Backend.

Key requirements:

  • Mutual TLS between SCC and backend for HTTPS propagation (backend must trust SCC system cert/CA chain).
  • CA Certificate configured in SCC can issue user certificate.
  • User mapping rules on AS Java (ClientCertLoginModule) to map certificate fields to UME users.
  1. Prerequisites Checklist

3.1 Backend user attribute alignment (SU01)

SAP Backend user email/user ID must match with BTP identity.

Ensure the email in the backend user record matches the email used to log in to BTP.

3.2 Cloud Connector certificates

You need three certificate

  1. System Certificate (for trust between SCC and backend)
  2. CA Certificate (to issue client certificate)
  3. User Sample Certificate (rule based mappting).

NOTE : SAP Supports Self-signed certificate for ABAP based systems, but Java not supports Self-signed and the SCC system certificate/Backend server certificate must be signed by CA.

Java Systems: Java-based systems (like SAP Portal or PI/PO) use a KeyStore-based trust model. While they can technically support self-signed certificates, But SAP's implementation for Principal Propagation via the Cloud Connector requires a Certificate Authority (CA) chain for validation.

3.3 Permissions & access

-SCC admin access

-BTP subaccount admin for Destinations

-AS Java admin access to NetWeaver Administrator (NWA) security and SSL configuration.

  1. Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1 - Maintain backend user mapping attribute (Email)

In backend user maintenance (ABAP SU01 / Java UME as applicable), fill E-Mail Address exactly as used in BTP.

Pick one mapping strategy (Email OR UserID) and use it consistently across BTP <-> IdP <->backend.

Here we taken Email strategy.

Step 2 - Generate/Import SCC certificates (System + CA + Sample User Cert)

2.1 System Certificate (SCC)

In Cloud Connector Admin UI:

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> System Certificate
  2. Create CSR and get it signed by CA (recommended).

  1. Import the certificate received from your AD team.

 

Also import the signed system certificate in AS Java trust store (NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>Trusted CA).

NOTE: Import chain certificate (binded=>actual certificate and RootCA).

2.2 CA Certificate (SCC)

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> CA Certificate
  2. CA Certificate can be self-signed or the same way you can generate CSR & import the signed CA certificate.

 

2.3 User Sample Certificate

Maintain subject pattern as email and generate the sample certificate.

 

Create sample certificate.

 

-Import the created sample certificate in NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>TicketKetstore

Step 3 - Sync Cloud Trust with IdP in SCC

Sync the trust configuration for principal propagation.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise -> Principal Propagation
  2. Click Sync to fetch IdP/trust configuration from the subaccount context.

 

Without this trust, SCC can’t validate the inbound identity and won’t be able to generate the user certificate.

Step 4 - Configure cloud to On-prem mapping Your backend system mapping must be configured for HTTPS and identity forwarding.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise → Access Control
  2. Create or edit your mapping for the backend system (HTTPS)
  3. Set Principal Type => X.509 Certificate
  4. Maintain resource paths (for example / or /sap/ depending on your requirement).

 

Set Principal Type = X.509 Certificate for the certificate-based identity.

 

Step 5 - Configure BTP Destination Authentication = PrincipalPropagation

In BTP:

  1. Navigate to Connectivity -> Destinations
  2. Create/update the Runtime destination to the backend via SCC
  3. Set:

Proxy Type: OnPremise

Type : HTTP

Authentication: PrincipalPropagation

URL points to virtual host/port from SCC mapping

  1. Backend Setup (SAP NetWeaver AS Java)

Step 6 - Activate HTTPS port in AS Java

AS Java must accept HTTPS and be able to request/require client certificates.

Configure SSL:

  1. Open: http://<host>:<port>/nwa     =>ssl
  2. Add a new access point with protocol HTTPS (ignore if HTTPS already active)

  1. Ensure SSL library is available and the SSL status becomes OK
  1. Secure/Apply SSL certificate for Java backend.

Your Java HTTPS endpoint certificate must be valid for the hostname used by SCC.

  1. Create/generate CSR for CA signing and import your CA response and root CA

     

Note : While generate the CSR don’t, forgot to maintain SAN (Subject Alternate Name) address, without SAN URL cannot be secured.

Tip: SAN can be FQDN and hostname of Java backend.(if you have two java instance generate CN = *.domain.com.

You can refer the SAP Note to generate the CSR & Certificate import using sapgenpse cmd line.

       2275562 - How to import sapgenpse generated PSE file into AS Java

Step 7 - Define Rule-Based Mapping (ClientCertLoginModule)

Converting the certificate identity into a UME user.

-Add ClientCertLoginModule

-Define rules for mapping certificate fields (Subject CN / SubjectAlternativeName/email) to a UME user.

NWA=>Authentication and Single Sign-On =>Authentication

Maintain below authentication stacks for Policy client_cert.

 

- Configure Clientcertloginmodule (Create Mapping rules)

-Enable Certificate Logon

-PrincipalMappingLoginModule

Step 8 – Define Reverse Proxy parameters

Add the parameter
icm/trusted_reverse_proxy_<X> = <SCC System Certificate Subject, Issuer>

This reverse proxy parameter used to establish the trust relationship between the SAP Backend and SAP Cloud connector.

NOTE : This is mandatory for Principal propagation.

Step 9 – Restart the Java services

  • Verify everything and restart the java services to make the parameters effect.

Step 10 – Validate the SSO functionality with the work zone application URL.

SUMMARY:
After completing the successful principal propagation setup as java backend, below are the recommendations.

  1. For Java based systems use the CA signed certificate.
  2. Ensure SAN entries are added in the SSL certificate.
  3. Generate sample certificate in SCC and import it in Ticketkeystore of NWA.
  4. Maintain only required fields => AttributeName, getUserFrom and UserMappingMode for ClientCertLoginModule.

 Thank you.

entry.content:

Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien, um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden

entry.title: Re: how can create an app variante using adapt UI and ass...
entry.content:
Good day @Hichem_Rahmani

Yes, your understanding is correct.

To reiterate, using spaces/pages only controls whether the app variant tile is shown on the user’s homepage. It does not automatically provide a hard authorisation control, so a user with the relevant app authorisation may still be able to find or start the app variant from the search bar.

If you need stricter control, you need to check whether your system supports IAM App Authorisation Variants for the app and then assign that only to your intended business roles.

See the reference below for more details on how to work with IAM Apps

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/e2b39fb01689420393931a9eb3f627ec.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b9fd81647cdf45f5ad462a50222c2caa.html?locale=en-US

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How BC Sets Work with Transport Requests in SAP
entry.content:
Business Configuration Sets (BC Sets) in SAP are tools used to capture and reuse Customizing (SPRO) configuration settings. They allow you to bundle configuration entries into a reusable object and move them across systems (DEV → QA → PRD) in a controlled way. 

BC Sets & Transport Request – How They Work Together 

  • When you create or record a BC Set (using T-code SCPR3), the system assigns it to a Transport Request. 
  • The BC Set definition (not the actual table entries) is saved in the transport. 
  • Once the Transport Request (SE10) is released, it can be imported into the target system. 
  • In the target system, the BC Set must be activated (SCPR20) to apply the configuration data to the tables.

 

When used? 

  • Template-based rollout projects 
  • S/4HANA migrations 
  • Multi-country implementations 
  • Reusable configuration packages

Taking example of this TR:

We'll create through TCode SCPR3

We'll give name following Z* and select type of BC set form (Transport Request).

After Activation and all we'll select particular BC Set and click on Download

After successful download we'll get below message. 

Now we'll select the Target System and Upload this file. (Transaction Code: SCPR3)

Here we'll click on upload option and select the correct downloaded file with ( .bcs ) extension.

Now we’ll activate it. (Transaction Code: SCPR20)

Conclusion: BC Sets are a structured way to capture, transport, and reuse SAP Customizing settings. While the Transport Request moves the BC Set object between systems, the actual configuration is applied only after activation in the target system. 

In short, BC Sets provide better control, reusability, and standardization of configuration compared to normal direct transports — especially useful in rollout and S/4HANA projects.

entry.title: Re: Displaying a Hidden Field from the Compensation Works...
entry.content:
Hi @Joe0603 

Since 2H 2025 you can make a field visible only on the worksheet, only visible on the profile, hidden on both or visible on both. But you can't have it hidden on the worksheet for a specific group of planners but visible on the profile for that same group.

Here are the tags you are looking for : hideInProfile="false" hideInForm="false"

 

Hope this helps.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Create a Transparent Table in SE11 (ABAP Dictionary)
entry.content:
In ABAP development, database tables form the backbone of application data storage. Among the different table types, transparent tables are the most commonly used because they directly map to database tables and store business data efficiently.

Understanding how to create a transparent table is a fundamental skill for every ABAP developer, especially for beginners who are starting with the ABAP Dictionary. In this blog, we will walk through a step-by-step approach to creating a simple transparent table using transaction SE11, along with an explanation of key concepts such as delivery class, data elements, domains, and technical settings.

By the end of this guide, you will have a clear understanding of the complete process and be able to create your own tables confidently.

In ABAP development, database tables are created using the ABAP Dictionary (SE11).
Transparent tables are used to store application data in the database.

1. Go to T Code SE11.

2. Enter database name as shown below.

Name should be start with Z or Y. In our case table name is ZDB_BANK.

3. Click on create.

4. Enter Short Description (It is mandatory).

5. Select delivery class (It is also mandatory).

Delivery Class: It ensures how data will transport between different SAP system landscapes. There are seven types of delivery class but mostly we use Application & Customizing.

6. Select Data browser/Table View Editing (It is mandatory).

Data browser/Table View Editing: It specifies whether maintenance is allowed or not.

7. Enter fields of the table and their data elements. We can also use built-in data type.

Built-in Type: They are standard data-type provided by SAP. Customization is very limited, like we cannot provide the field label for the column. Also, we cannot use domain in this case.

Data Element: It specifies the semantic characteristics of the field.

Steps to create Data Element:

  • Enter the desired data element name (starting with Z or Y) in the column – data element.
  • Double clicks on the name and click on create.
  • Enter the short description & field label.

 

  • Enter the domain name or built-in type.

Domain: It specifies the technical characteristics of the field like value range or data-type etc.

  • Enter the domain name and double clicks on it.
  • Enter short description and data-type.

  • Click on activate

  • Press esc button.
  • Activate data element.
  • Press esc and you will redirect to the table.
  • Once done with all the fields, click on Technical Setting.

Technical Setting: It specifies the technical aspects of the table like data class, size, function group etc.

Data Class: It specifies which type of data this table will store. For example, APP0 for master data and APP1 for transaction data.

Size Category: It is the size of the lot assigned for the table in the memory at a time.
It is used for reservation of initial memory space in the database.

  • Create and save.

Steps to create entries:

  • Click on utilities.

  • Click on create entries under Table Contents

Steps to display entries:

  • Click on table icon

  • Entries will display.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we explored the end-to-end process of creating a transparent table in the ABAP Dictionary using SE11. We covered not only the technical steps but also the purpose of important components like data elements, domains, delivery class, and technical settings.

Creating database tables is one of the foundational tasks in ABAP development, and mastering these basics helps build a strong understanding of how SAP applications store and manage data.

I hope this step-by-step guide helps beginners gain confidence in working with the ABAP Dictionary. If you have any questions, suggestions, or additional tips, feel free to share them in the comments — I’d be happy to discuss and learn together.

If you found this helpful, feel free to connect with me.
I will be posting more beginner-friendly ABAP blogs regularly.
